query_analysis:
**Summary of the Query:**
Determine the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft and its primary material composition.

**Skills and Tools Needed:**

1. **Research Skills:**
   - Necessary for understanding and verifying information about aircrafts, their speeds, and materials.
   
2. **Web Search Capabilities:**
   - To find reliable sources and data about the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft and its construction materials.

**Explanation:**

- **Research Skills:** Essential for comprehending the context and evaluating the credibility of information found.
- **Web Search Capabilities (Ground_Google_Search_Tool, Web_RAG_Search_Tool, Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool):** 
  - **Ground_Google_Search_Tool** can provide real-time information and updates about the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft and its materials.
  - **Web_RAG_Search_Tool** and **Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool** are useful for retrieving detailed and structured information from specific websites or Wikipedia, ensuring accuracy and relevance.

**Additional Considerations:**
- Verify the information from multiple sources to ensure accuracy.
- Consider the age of the data, as advancements in aircraft technology can quickly change the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft.
- Use specialized tools to cross-verify materials used in aircraft construction, as this can vary based on the specific model and era.

- **Memory (Tools Used & Results):** {'Action Step 1': {'tool_name': 'Ground_Google_Search_Tool', 'sub_goal': 'Retrieve the name of the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft and its primary material composition from a reliable source.', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="What is the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft mostly made out of?")', 'result': ['The fastest air-breathing manned aircraft is the Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird, and it is mostly made out of titanium.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [2](https://boomsupersonic.com/flyby/museum-of-flight-reveals-secrets-of-the-sr-71-blackbird), [3](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEXLlgcTn2tazENp19wsjZt6uXc7PfxMzy2Tmnt5NQ_IMXCL4kSSDoTkXVry9zYFqvbuvok42yEg7F0XuxesDJxKWzo2UeqyR97nIHyvYGWQTCTGzpMQnhWPWL6htcZgUyyuAGHX3uAF_Neifk2x1cJ8PL36Wei9p-Hp0cMwjGDg0f1rSt_UIDZGXDQOCtDd03YSizAZwCPp0LmAnINk3HZFPybErudd8HycAE7AZtrQiSEyOk-Qy-2vu8ZbtskEpD0xCs0hDjNaicSBOaDiH25), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926), [5](https://airandspace.si.edu/collection-objects/lockheed-sr-71-blackbird/nasm_A19920072000), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEiNq1hbcxZpSRsTgG6qXe-mE71Ne2a6nZca2b9lqwxdy3ckHMWT0Pcm-0PW_WpQL5vlX_gjrVa2G2Domflo8h-Ad0OuPjN0HEy9alblbPvm_dlNVVxh_xCAHMT3etdzxPDNuoVKU_7r9TCbFCMarneSQ==)

The SR-71 Blackbird holds world records for both speed and altitude in air-breathing manned flight, reaching speeds of Mach 3.3 (approximately 2,193 mph or 3,529.6 km/h) and altitudes of over 85,000 feet.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lockheed_SR-71_Blackbird), [8](https://simpleflying.com/fastest-aircraft-ever-built/) Its airframe was constructed with titanium alloys, making up over 85% to 93% of its structure.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [9](http://sr71.us/sr_construction.htm), [10](https://simpleflying.com/why-lockheed-build-sr-71-titanium/), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEiNq1hbcxZpSRsTgG6qXe-mE71Ne2a6nZca2b9lqwxdy3ckHMWT0Pcm-0PW_WpQL5vlX_gjrVa2G2Domflo8h-Ad0OuPjN0HEy9alblbPvm_dlNVVxh_xCAHMT3etdzxPDNuoVKU_7r9TCbFCMarneSQ==) This extensive use of titanium was crucial due to the extreme heat generated by sustained high-speed flight, as titanium retains its strength at temperatures where aluminum would soften.[3](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEXLlgcTn2tazENp19wsjZt6uXc7PfxMzy2Tmnt5NQ_IMXCL4kSSDoTkXVry9zYFqvbuvok42yEg7F0XuxesDJxKWzo2UeqyR97nIHyvYGWQTCTGzpMQnhWPWL6htcZgUyyuAGHX3uAF_Neifk2x1cJ8PL36Wei9p-Hp0cMwjGDg0f1rSt_UIDZGXDQOCtDd03YSizAZwCPp0LmAnINk3HZFPybErudd8HycAE7AZtrQiSEyOk-Qy-2vu8ZbtskEpD0xCs0hDjNaicSBOaDiH25), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926) The remaining portion of the aircraft was composed of polymer composite materials.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [2](https://boomsupersonic.com/flyby/museum-of-flight-reveals-secrets-of-the-sr-71-blackbird)']}}

Action Step 1.command: execution = tool.execute(query="What is the fastest air-breathing manned aircraft mostly made out of?")
result:
The fastest air-breathing manned aircraft is the Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird, and it is mostly made out of titanium.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [2](https://boomsupersonic.com/flyby/museum-of-flight-reveals-secrets-of-the-sr-71-blackbird), [3](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEXLlgcTn2tazENp19wsjZt6uXc7PfxMzy2Tmnt5NQ_IMXCL4kSSDoTkXVry9zYFqvbuvok42yEg7F0XuxesDJxKWzo2UeqyR97nIHyvYGWQTCTGzpMQnhWPWL6htcZgUyyuAGHX3uAF_Neifk2x1cJ8PL36Wei9p-Hp0cMwjGDg0f1rSt_UIDZGXDQOCtDd03YSizAZwCPp0LmAnINk3HZFPybErudd8HycAE7AZtrQiSEyOk-Qy-2vu8ZbtskEpD0xCs0hDjNaicSBOaDiH25), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926), [5](https://airandspace.si.edu/collection-objects/lockheed-sr-71-blackbird/nasm_A19920072000), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEiNq1hbcxZpSRsTgG6qXe-mE71Ne2a6nZca2b9lqwxdy3ckHMWT0Pcm-0PW_WpQL5vlX_gjrVa2G2Domflo8h-Ad0OuPjN0HEy9alblbPvm_dlNVVxh_xCAHMT3etdzxPDNuoVKU_7r9TCbFCMarneSQ==)

The SR-71 Blackbird holds world records for both speed and altitude in air-breathing manned flight, reaching speeds of Mach 3.3 (approximately 2,193 mph or 3,529.6 km/h) and altitudes of over 85,000 feet.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lockheed_SR-71_Blackbird), [8](https://simpleflying.com/fastest-aircraft-ever-built/) Its airframe was constructed with titanium alloys, making up over 85% to 93% of its structure.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [9](http://sr71.us/sr_construction.htm), [10](https://simpleflying.com/why-lockheed-build-sr-71-titanium/), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926), [6](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEiNq1hbcxZpSRsTgG6qXe-mE71Ne2a6nZca2b9lqwxdy3ckHMWT0Pcm-0PW_WpQL5vlX_gjrVa2G2Domflo8h-Ad0OuPjN0HEy9alblbPvm_dlNVVxh_xCAHMT3etdzxPDNuoVKU_7r9TCbFCMarneSQ==) This extensive use of titanium was crucial due to the extreme heat generated by sustained high-speed flight, as titanium retains its strength at temperatures where aluminum would soften.[3](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEXLlgcTn2tazENp19wsjZt6uXc7PfxMzy2Tmnt5NQ_IMXCL4kSSDoTkXVry9zYFqvbuvok42yEg7F0XuxesDJxKWzo2UeqyR97nIHyvYGWQTCTGzpMQnhWPWL6htcZgUyyuAGHX3uAF_Neifk2x1cJ8PL36Wei9p-Hp0cMwjGDg0f1rSt_UIDZGXDQOCtDd03YSizAZwCPp0LmAnINk3HZFPybErudd8HycAE7AZtrQiSEyOk-Qy-2vu8ZbtskEpD0xCs0hDjNaicSBOaDiH25), [4](https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/why-america-built-only-one-plane-out-of-titanium-hk-011926) The remaining portion of the aircraft was composed of polymer composite materials.[1](https://tmstitanium.com/news-post/the-lockheed-sr-71-blackbird), [2](https://boomsupersonic.com/flyby/museum-of-flight-reveals-secrets-of-the-sr-71-blackbird)

direct_output: <answer>The fastest air-breathing manned aircraft, the Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird, is mostly made out of titanium, which constitutes over 85% to 93% of its structure. The remaining portion of the aircraft is composed of polymer composite materials.</answer>
